transliteration: asatyam apratiṣṭhaṃ te jagad āhur anīśvaram | aparaspara-sambhūtaṃ kim anyat kāma-haitukam ||
literal_meaning: They say the world is unreal, without foundation, without a controller; born of mutual desire, nothing more than lust.
contextual_meaning: Krishna describes the cynical, materialistic worldview that dissolves meaning, purpose, and moral order, reducing life to random chemistry and selfish appetite.
psychological_state: Existential vacuum—defensive intellectualization that strips cosmos of value to avoid vulnerability and accountability.
trigger_scenario: After a breakup you tell yourself 'love is just hormones, nothing sacred,' and scroll dating apps to prove it.
anchor_text: Life is more than random chemistry; seek meaning beyond selfish appetite.
